title: Psychotria caerulea
taxonomy: Psychotria caerulea is classified within the family Rubiaceae, which is a large and diverse family of flowering plants commonly known as the coffee or madder family. This family consists of over 600 genera and more than 13,000 species. Psychotria caerulea is further classified within the genus Psychotria, which is native to the tropical regions of the world and contains around 150 species. The genus Psychotria is often referred to as the wild coffee or wild forest coffee due to its resemblance to coffee plants. The classification of Psychotria caerulea is a part of the broader system of plant taxonomy, which helps to organize and understand the diversity of plant species.

intro: Psychotria caerulea, also known as Blue Psychotria or Blue Angel, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may aid in reducing stress and promoting overall well-being. The bioactive constituents of Psychotria caerulea include alkaloids such as psychotridine and isopsychotridine, as well as flavonoids and phenolic compounds. Traditionally, the plant is used to make herbal teas and tinctures, which are used to promote relaxation, improve sleep quality, and support immune function.
